query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.186 (commit: d6dd8b51). ## Issue to Resolve [BUG]: Can't display images with `#` in filenames ### Checked for duplicates? - [X] This issue is not a duplicate ### What are the steps to reproduce this bug? 1. Add a card including an image file containing `#` (hashtag) in its filename --- --- --- --- 3. Show the card in reviewer or previewer ### Expected behaviour The image is shown, as on 2.16.5. (2.16.5) ### Actual behaviour The broken-image icon is shown, and the image isn't shown. ### Debug info ```text AnkiDroid Version = 2.17.6 (568538896be943818ca793dd2635822265712def) Backend Version = 0.1.34-anki23.12.1 (23.12.1 1a1d4d5419c6b57ef3baf99c9d2d9cf85d36ae0a) Android Version = 11 (SDK 30) ProductFlavor = play Manufacturer = OPPO Model = CPH2013 Hardware = qcom Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 11; CPH2013 Build/RKQ1.200903.002; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/122.0.6261.119 Mobile Safari/537.36 ACRA UUID = df169a4d-29ec-46b6-b114-141ca7e01ba3 Crash Reports Enabled = true ``` ### (Optional) Anything else you want to share? - Originally reported in Anki Forum: https://forums.ankiweb.net/t/ankiweb-and-ankidroid-do-not-display-images-containing-pound-hashtag-sharp-symbol/42444/1 and dae has commented there. - As reported there, this issue occurs on AnkiWeb, too. ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/c588f585-b23c-4be6-b8f1-8a098a127705) So, strictly speaking, this is not a bug specific to AnkiDroid, but some approach specific to AnkiDroid may be needed, I'm not sure though. - This issue doesn't occur on Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum) and on AnkiMobile. - The `#` is shown as `%23` on the editor of Anki Desktop (as reported in Anki Forum). ![image](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/assets/10436072/5ba75763-6007-4668-b036-0cb13e54cdc5) > \> Windows changes it back to a # in the sort field, but the HTML text remains unchanged > > This happens because Anki stores filenames without encoding on disk (legacy reasons), but needs to encode them before they’re shown in the editor. (dae) ### Research - [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app) - [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue - [X] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting)) (2.18alpha6) ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion It seems to have already been fixed in the current HEAD. I don't think so, it is ongoing discussion in the PR, https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/15909#issuecomment-2001940011 Thank you for the feedback. Not for objection (since I don't have enough knowledge about the topic), just for rewording more accurately and for what it is worth, the issue seems to have been fixed in my debug version build from a854e93 (Mar 17, 2024). On the other hand, a card with the image file added in Anki Desktop is not shown properly (i.e. shown as a broken-image icon) even in the debug version of AnkiDroid. In this case, `#` is shown as it is in the editor and the browser. “#” is a illegal char in HTML, so it’s not supposed to work anywhere if not encoded. Ankidroid encodes it when importing, and AFAIK Anki does it as well. > Ankidroid encodes it when importing In case that users import cards that contain image files with unencoded `#`, for example, importing the following shared deck, why images with `#` in filenames are shown in reviewer on 2.16.5, but not on 2.17.6? - https://ankiweb.net/shared/info/789434294 --- Read now damien's comment about "legacy reasons". Since `#` still isn't supposed to be treated as something else that isn't a URL fragment marker in that context, this is a deliberate anki breakage of the URL scheme. I hate breaking things deliberately without a unavoidable reason, but I'll send the fix ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Let me understand this issue:

1. Images with `#` (hashtag) in filenames cannot be displayed in AnkiDroid version 2.17.6
2. In version 2.16.5, these images were displayed correctly
3. When images are added via AnkiDroid, they work (because AnkiDroid encodes the `#` to `%23`)
4. When images are added via Anki Desktop, the `#` is stored as `%23` in the HTML but the actual filename on disk has `#`
5. When importing cards with unencoded `#` in filenames, they don't display in 2.17.6 but did in 2.16.5

The issue is that `#` is a special character in URLs (it marks URL fragments), so when a filename contains `#`, it needs to be URL-encoded as `%23` in the HTML src attribute.

The key insight from the discussion is:
- AnkiDroid 2.16.5 could display these images somehow
- AnkiDroid 2.17.6 cannot
- Something changed between these versions that broke the handling of `#` in filenames

The relevant files mentioned are:
- `.idea/dictionaries/davidallison.xml` - probably just a dictionary file, not relevant to the fix
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/TemplateManager.kt` - this might be where rendering happens

I need to:
1. Find where image URLs are processed/handled in AnkiDroid
2. Look for changes between 2.16.5 and 2.17.6 that might have broken this
3. Implement a fix that properly handles `#` in filenames by encoding them to `%23`

Let me start by exploring the codebase to understand how images are handled.

content.thinking: I can see the issue! In line 51, the code does: ```kotlin val file = File(mediaDir, path) ``` The `path` comes from `url.path` (line 41), which is the decoded path from the URL. The issue is that when an HTML contains ``, the URL parsing treats `#name.jpg` as a fragment identifier (the part after `#` in URLs), not as part of the filename. When Anki Desktop encodes the filename to `%23`, the URL path will be `/file%23name.jpg`, which when decoded becomes `/file#name.jpg`, which is correct. But when the HTML contains an unencoded `#`, like ``, the URL parser sees: - path: `/file` - fragment: `name.jpg` So `url.path` returns just `/file` instead of `/file#name.jpg`. The fix should be to also check the fragment and append it to the path with `#` if it exists. This would handle legacy cards that have unencoded `#` in their HTML. Let me check if there's a fragment property in the URL object.

- `url.path` returns: `/image`
- `url.fragment` returns: `test.jpg`

But the actual filename is `image#test.jpg`.

The fix should append the fragment back to the path with a `#` when trying to find the file. Let me implement this fix.

This affected: - Legacy cards imported from shared decks with unencoded `#` in image filenames - Cards created in environments where `#` wasn't properly URL-encoded ## Root Cause When HTML contains an unencoded `#` character in an image src attribute (e.g., ``), the URL parser treats everything after the `#` as a URL fragment identifier, not as part of the filename: - `url.path` returns `/image` - `url.fragment` returns `test.jpg` The code in `ViewerResourceHandler.kt` was only using `url.path` to construct the file path, resulting in looking for a file named `image` instead of `image#test.jpg`. ## Solution Modified `ViewerResourceHandler.shouldInterceptRequest()` to reconstruct the full filename by concatenating the URL path and fragment when a fragment exists: ```kotlin val path = if (url.fragment != null) { "$urlPath#${url.fragment}" } else { urlPath } ``` This fix: 1. Handles legacy cards with unencoded `#` in filenames 2. Continues to work with properly encoded filenames (`%23`) 3. Doesn't affect normal filenames without `#` 4. Handles edge cases like multiple `#` characters ## Files Changed - `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - Main fix - `/project/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandlerTest.kt` - New test file ## Tests Created comprehensive unit tests covering: - Normal filenames (without `#`) - Filenames with unencoded `#` in URL fragments - Filenames with properly encoded `#` (`%23`) - Multiple `#` characters - Non-existent files - Wrong authority All tests pass successfully.  19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.webkit.WebResourceRequest
 21→import android.webkit.WebResourceResponse
 22→import com.ichi2.utils.AssetHelper.guessMimeType
 23→import timber.log.Timber
 24→import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25→import java.io.File
 26→import java.io.FileInputStream
 27→import java.io.InputStream
 28→import java.io.RandomAccessFile
 29→import java.nio.channels.Channels
 30→
 31→private const val RANGE_HEADER = "Range"
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * @param authority the authority of the WebView Url, e.g. `127.0.0.1:40001`.
 35→ */
 36→class ViewerResourceHandler(context: Context, private val authority: String) {
 37→    private val mediaDir = CollectionHelper.getMediaDirectory(context)
 38→
 39→    fun shouldInterceptRequest(request: WebResourceRequest): WebResourceResponse? {
 40→        val url = request.url
 41→        val path = url.path
 42→
 43→        if (request.method != "GET" || path == null || url.authority != authority) {
 44→            return null
 45→        }
 46→        if (path == "/favicon.ico") {
 47→            return WebResourceResponse(null, null, ByteArrayInputStream(ByteArray(0)))
 48→        }
 49→
 50→        try {
 51→            val file = File(mediaDir, path)
 52→            if (!file.exists()) {
 53→                return null
 54→            }
 55→            request.requestHeaders[RANGE_HEADER]?.let { range ->
 56→                return handlePartialContent(file, range)
 57→            }
 58→            val inputStream = FileInputStream(file)
 59→            return WebResourceResponse(guessMimeType(path), null, inputStream)
 60→        } catch (e: Exception) {
 61→            Timber.d("File not found")
 62→            return null
 63→        }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun handlePartialContent(file: File, range: String): WebResourceResponse {
 67→        val rangeHeader = RangeHeader.from(range, defaultEnd = file.length() - 1)
 68→
 69→        val mimeType = guessMimeType(file.path)
 70→        val inputStream = file.toInputStream(rangeHeader)
 71→        val (start, end) = rangeHeader
 72→        val responseHeaders = mapOf(
 73→            "Content-Range" to "bytes $start-$end/${file.length()}",
 74→            "Accept-Range" to "bytes"
 75→        )
 76→        return WebResourceResponse(
 77→            mimeType,
 78→            null,
 79→            206,
 80→            "Partial Content",
 81→            responseHeaders,
 82→            inputStream
 83→        )
 84→    }
 85→}
 86→
 87→/**
 88→ * Handles the "range" header in a HTTP Request
 89→ */
 90→data class RangeHeader(val start: Long, val end: Long) {
 91→    companion object {
 92→        fun from(range: String, defaultEnd: Long): RangeHeader {
 93→            val numbers = range.substring("bytes=".length).split('-')
 94→            val unspecifiedEnd = numbers.getOrNull(1).isNullOrEmpty()
 95→            return RangeHeader(
 96→                start = numbers[0].toLong(),
 97→                end = if (unspecifiedEnd) defaultEnd else numbers[1].toLong()
 98→            )
 99→        }
100→    }
101→}
102→
103→fun File.toInputStream(header: RangeHeader): InputStream {
104→    // PERF: Test to see if a custom FileInputStream + available() would be faster
105→    val randomAccessFile = RandomAccessFile(this, "r")
106→    return Channels.newInputStream(randomAccessFile.channel).also {
107→        it.skip(header.start)
108→    }
109→}

Handles legacy cards with unencoded `#` in filenames 2. Continues to work with properly encoded filenames (`%23`) 3. Doesn't affect normal filenames without `#` 4. Handles edge cases like multiple `#` characters ## Files Changed - `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandler.kt` - Main fix - `/project/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerResourceHandlerTest.kt` - New test file ## Tests Created comprehensive unit tests covering: - Normal filenames (without `#`) - Filenames with unencoded `#` in URL fragments - Filenames with properly encoded `#` (`%23`) - Multiple `#` characters - Non-existent files - Wrong authority All tests pass successfully. EOF cat /tmp/fix_summary.md", "explanation": "Creating a summary of the fix"}
